Author: Michelle Xie
Title: I made an app using GitHub Spark in 2 hours
Text:
Member-only story
I made an app using GitHub Spark in 2 hours
Last week I heard about GitHub Spark for the first time which is something that doesn’t come as a surprise to any of us, especially as a developer. Even before the age of Generative A.I, we had tools like Power Apps that encouraged non-coders to build apps by giving them a drag-and-drop interface that could piece together logic and allow them to publish an app all through a user interface (U.I).
So it was no surprise to any of us with Generative A.I and agentic A.I that soon there would be a new way to allow us to write code, build apps and design solutions.
It is common knowledge these days that even non-coders can build an app without any development background. They even coined this trend, ‘vibe coding’.
You’ve probably seen the ads. Pictures of what I can only best describe as ‘scribbles’ taken on a camera and uploaded to Generative A.I models and then an app is generated from it. I’ve used Generative A.I in my own coding job as a developer to help me write code because it does do a good job, until it doesn’t. Why? Because Generative A.I is not perfect.
Well, neither are humans.
Most humans are inherently lazy, I consider myself to be one of those lazy humans. I’ve had ideas, my friends have ideas, yes, we all have ideas. These ideas usually start with, “Let’s…
